summary: The main claim is: AI is capable of ending the human race, in fact I suspect that it does that by default.

In his post on the topic, user states that a good responsible scaling policy could cut the risks from AI by a factor of 10: I believe that a very good RSP (of the kind I've been advocating for) could cut risk dramatically if implemented ef…
The main claim is: It sometimes happens that people who I've talked to or worked with ask me where they should go for financial support for their research.
I haven't developed a standard list of answers to this question.
The main claim is: I introduce the notion of design paradigms, when discussing design and engineering.
And therefore in order to understand what paradigms govern progress in AI, and how they do so, we need to look at paradigms not just in science but in design and engineering.
The main claim is: If the latter is necessary, the number of different lotteries used in the EPEs should be as low as possible.
This hypothetical collectice decision should be as fair as possible, trying as best as possible not to sacrifice one stakeholder's preferences for the sake of others.
The main claim is: Research questions in this language: When and why does a model have preferences about its inputs?
Your RL agent has an observed preference for coins but these may not be the internal preferences.
The main claim is: I think humans would rather be happier or smarter than simply more.
I have no idea if this background makes me more or less likely to generate a new idea that's useful to solving AI alignment, but the way I understand the problem now: everyone should at least try.
The main claim is: We take a stand against capabilities externalities in some safety research directions.
One objective of AI safety research should be to improve scaling laws of safety relative to capabilities.
The main claim is: Of the 786 questions, the best performing prompt made legible predictions for 770.
To answer this question I had GPT-3 predict the likelihood for every binary question ever resolved on Metaculus.
